question: How do I connect the camera using a household power socket?
content:
Use the optional AC Adapter Kit ACK-E2. 1. Connect the DC Coupler plug to the AC adapter socket.<PIC>
2. Connect the power cord to the AC adapter, then insert the plug into the household power outlet.<PIC>
3. Carefully place the cord in the groove without damaging it.<PIC>
4. Open the battery compartment cover and the DC Coupler cord notch cover. Insert the DC Coupler until it locks, route the cord through the notch, then close the cover.<PIC>
Do not connect or disconnect the power cord while the camera's power switch is set to <ON> or the Quick Control Dial position.
ret:

2. Connect the power cord to the AC adapter, then insert the plug into the household power outlet.<PIC>
3. Carefully place the cord in the groove without damaging it.<PIC>
4. Open the battery compartment cover and the DC Coupler cord notch cover. Insert the DC Coupler until it locks, route the cord through the notch, then close the cover.<PIC>
Do not connect or disconnect the power cord while the camera's power switch is set to <ON> or the Quick Control Dial position.",["Camera_14", "Camera_15", "Manual10_19", "Manual10_20"]
notes: Answer grounded in the Canon EOS 20D household power outlet procedure. Four adjacent figures are included because each supports a distinct physical connection or routing step.
